title: 10 best herbal tinctures for infection
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for infection are concentrated plant extracts used to combat bacterial, viral, or fungal infections. These natural remedies work by stimulating the body's immune system to fight off pathogens, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with infection include goldenseal, which combats bacterial infections such as UTIs, and echinacea, which boosts the immune system against viral infections like the common cold. By using these tinctures, individuals can alleviate symptoms and recover faster from illnesses, improving their overall quality of life.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: The tincture contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against a range of bacteria and viruses, helping to combat infections.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and inflammation associated with infections and promoting healing.

Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower contains immunomodulatory compounds that help stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off infections and reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with infection because they contain potent compounds like echinacin, which has been shown to have antimicrobial properties. These properties help to combat a wide range of microorganisms, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi. Additionally, Kansas coneflower tinctures also possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant qualities, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting overall immune system function. By stimulating the body's natural defenses, Kansas coneflower tinctures can provide effective relief from infection and promote a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea angustifolia have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the immune system's response to infection.
Iridoid glycosides (e.g., echinacoside): Iridoid glycosides have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation associated with infections.
Caffeic acid phenylethyl ester (CAPE): CAPE is a phenolic compound that has been demonstrated to exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to inhibit the growth of pathogens and prevent infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are commonly used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Stems: The stems of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make tinctures because they contain a significant amount of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: The barks of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make tinctures due to their rich content of phenolic compounds, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall or late summer season.
Chop 1 part of the roots into smaller pieces weighing approximately 1 pound or 450 grams.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 parts of 80-100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily for 2 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: King of bitters tinctures helps with infection because it contains a potent blend of herbs that work synergistically to combat bacterial, viral, and fungal invaders. The antimicrobial properties of ingredients like goldenseal, echinacea, and burdock root help to disrupt the replication cycle of pathogens, reducing their ability to spread and cause harm. Additionally, the tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe the immune system, promoting a healthy response to infection and accelerating the body's natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: King of Bitters contains compounds like berberine, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, helping to combat infection-causing pathogens.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The bitter compounds in King of Bitters, such as quercetin, can reduce inflammation, which is often a contributing factor to infections and can help to alleviate symptoms like pain and swelling.

Immune system support: King of Bitters contains adaptogenic compounds that can enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms, boosting the immune system's ability to respond to and fight off infections.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: A diterpenoid lactone that exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections by inhibiting the growth and replication of pathogens.

Andrographolide glucoside: A glycosidic derivative of andrographolide that contributes to the herb's immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, thereby enhancing the body's natural defense against infections.

Lithospermic acid: A phenylethnolide that displays potent antimicrobial activity, particularly against Gram-positive bacteria, by disrupting cell membrane integrity and inhibiting the production of virulence factors.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Andrographis paniculata are used to make tinctures for infection because they contain the highest concentration of the bioactive compound andrographolide, which has antimicrobial properties.
Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures for infection due to their ability to release andrographolide and other compounds that help combat bacterial and fungal infections.
Stems: The stems are used to make tinctures for infection because they contain andrographolide and other flavonoids that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 cups of andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly with water.
Combine the clean andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the andrographis paniculata tincture in a cool dark place and use it within 2 years, dosing 20 to 30 drops per serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with infection because of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi. The tannins present in licorice root have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against various pathogens, including herpes simplex virus and influenza virus. Additionally, licorice's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and promote healing in infected areas. By targeting both the microorganism causing the infection and reducing inflammation, herbal licorice tinctures can provide a natural and holistic approach to combating infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with infections such as swelling, pain, and redness.

Antimicrobial properties: Licorice root has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against a range of microorganisms, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi, helping to prevent the spread of infection and support the body's natural healing processes.

Saponins: Licorice root is rich in saponins, which have been found to enhance the immune system's response to infections by stimulating the production of white blood cells and increasing their activity against pathogens.
remedy_constituents: Liquiritin: It helps with infection by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various pathogens such as bacteria and fungi.
Glycyrrhizin: It helps with infection by showing antiviral and antibacterial activities, potentially reducing the severity and duration of infections.
Liquorice Isoflavones: It helps with infection by displaying antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation often associated with infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used to make tinctures for infection due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for infection due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects.
Roots: The roots are also used for their saponin content, which has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity and aid in wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 dry roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in the morning after the dew has dried for maximum potency.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase the surface area for solvent absorption using a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped root pieces with 70% ethanol in a 1:3 ratio by weight in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the container every day or two.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric tinctures helps with infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which allow it to effectively combat a wide range of microorganisms. The curcuminoids present in turmeric have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, while also reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. This makes tree turmeric tinctures an excellent natural remedy for infections such as sinusitis, bronchitis, and ear infections, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's antimicrobial activity: Turmeric tincture contains curcumin, a compound that inhibits the growth of many types of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, helping to reduce infection severity.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with infections, such as pain and discomfort, by decreasing pro-inflammatory molecules in the body.
Immunomodulatory activity: Turmeric tincture has been shown to stimulate the immune system, enhancing its ability to fight off infections and promote a faster recovery from illness.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine, an alkaloid found in Berberis aristata, exhibits antimicrobial properties by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi, thereby helping to combat infections.
Isovitexin: Isovitexin, a flavonoid glycoside, has been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing the severity of infections and promoting healing.

Vitexin: Vitexin, another flavonoid glycoside present in Berberis aristata, has been found to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in protecting against infections and promoting overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a compound with antimicrobial properties that helps combat infections.
Barks: Contain berberine and other alkaloids, contributing to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects in treating infections.
Leaves: Also rich in berberine, which helps to inhibit the growth of microorganisms and reduce inflammation in the body.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of berberis aristata roots and 2 parts of apple cider vinegar in a clean glass jar.
Add 80 proof vodka to the jar to cover the roots completely for 4-6 weeks.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it 2-3 times a day for 4 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Discard the solids and transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with infection because they contain powerful antimicrobial compounds that target invading pathogens, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The tincture's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work synergistically to destroy bacteria, viruses, and fungi, while also soothing the affected area. Additionally, elder tinctures have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with infection, allowing the body to recover more effectively.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Elder tincture contains antiviral compounds like sambucin, which inhibit viral replication and reduce the severity of symptoms associated with viral infections such as flu and colds.

Antibacterial and antimicrobial properties: Elder tincture has been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antimicrobial properties, making it effective against a range of bacterial and fungal infections, including sinusitis, bronchitis, and urinary tract infections.

Immune system stimulants: The sambucus plant contains flavonoids and polyphenols that stimulate the immune system, helping to boost the body's natural defenses against infection and reduce inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds possess potent antimicrobial properties, helping to combat the growth and spread of infectious microorganisms.
Sambunigrin: This glycoside has been shown to have antiviral activity, inhibiting the replication of viruses and thereby reducing the risk of infection.

Ellagic acid: As a phenolic compound, ellagic acid exhibits antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress that can contribute to infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high content of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Used for their antiseptic and antiviral properties, which help combat infections and promote wound healing.
Stems: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and fighting off infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and berries in mid to late summer when they are fully ripe and dry.
Clean the sambucus nigra flowers and berries thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris using a fine mesh sieve.
Combine 1 part sambucus nigra flowers and berries with 2 parts high-proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with infection because of its potent antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, including taraxasterol and taraxanthin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including E. coli, Staphylococcus aureus, and Candida albicans. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce swelling and pain associated with infected areas, allowing the body's natural healing processes to take over more efficiently. As a result, herbal dandelion tinctures can be an effective natural remedy for supporting immune function and combating infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Dandelion tincture has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which helps to combat and prevent the growth of harmful bacteria, viruses, and fungi responsible for infections.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids present in dandelion tincture have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with infections, promoting a faster healing process.

Immune system stimulation: Dandelion tincture contains immunomodulatory compounds that stimulate the immune system, helping to increase its response to infection and fight off invading pathogens more effectively.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene ester helps with infection by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, particularly against certain bacteria and fungi, thereby inhibiting their growth and preventing the spread of infection.
Inulin: A type of fructan, inulin has prebiotic properties, which help to promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, thereby boosting the immune system and reducing the risk of infection.

Taraxasterol acetate (Taraxerone): This triterpene has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of pathogens that can cause infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are rich in saponins, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat infections.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating infections.
Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale contain apigenin, a flavonoid with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves and roots from the plant in the early morning when moisture is high.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and place it in a clean glass jar.
Add 500ml of 35% ethanol to the jar and seal it tightly for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle and keep it in a cool place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with infection because its active compound, hyperforin, has potent antimicrobial properties. This allows it to effectively combat a wide range of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause infections. Hyperforin has been shown to inhibit the growth of pathogens, reducing their ability to multiply and spread, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of infection such as pain, inflammation, and fever. Additionally, St John's Wort tinctures may also help boost the body's natural immune response, enabling it to fight off infections more effectively.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: St John's Wort tincture has been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, helping to combat infections caused by viral pathogens such as herpes and HIV.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and hyperforin present in St John's Wort tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with infections and inflammation.

Immunomodulatory activity: St John's Wort tincture has been found to modulate the immune system by stimulating the production of white blood cells and activating natural killer cells, helping the body to fight off infections more effectively.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This prenylated phenolic compound exhibits antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which help combat bacterial and viral infections.

Naphthodianthrones: Flavonoids such as hypericin and pseudohypericin within the naphthodianthrones class display antiviral and antibacterial activities, contributing to the plant's infection-fighting properties.
Flavonoids: Specifically, rutin and hyperoside, two flavonoid glycosides found in Hypericum perforatum, possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that aid in infection management.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain hypericin and hyperforin, which have antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: They contain alkaloids like hypericin and hyperforin, which have antimicrobial and antiviral properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves in late spring or early summer when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 to 2 weeks.
Measure out 1 part of the dried plant material and combine it with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with infection because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids present in pot marigold have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, thereby reducing the severity of infections. remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help with infection by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and inhibiting the growth of microorganisms.

Phenylpropanoids (specifically, caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid): These phenolic compounds help with infection by displaying antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which help to neutralize free radicals and prevent the proliferation of pathogens.

Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial properties to create a tincture that helps prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi, reducing the risk of infection.
Stems: Not commonly used but, some use the stems to create a tincture that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, similar to the flowers.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh calendula officinalis flowers or 100 grams of dried flowers for making tinctures.
Combine the gathered flowers with 1 liter of 25% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the resulting tincture to small dark glass bottles for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Leafy spurge tinctures helps with infection because they contain a unique blend of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit potent antimicrobial properties. These compounds have been found to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi, thereby helping to combat infections and promote healing. remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Euphorbia esula tinctures have antimicrobial properties, helping to inhibit the growth of various bacteria and fungi, thereby reducing the risk of infection.

remedy_recipe: Gather euphorbia esula roots and stems for tincture preparation weighing 1 pound total.
Chop euphorbia esula plant material into small pieces then combine with 5 cups of 80 proof vodka.
Allow the euphorbia esula vodka mixture to steep in a glass jar for 4 to 6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the euphorbia esula tincture through cheesecloth into a separate container discarding solids.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat infections.
Stems: The stems of Phyllanthus niruri contain flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective against infections.
Roots: The roots of the plant are used for their ability to exhibit antimicrobial properties, specifically against E. coli and Staphylococcus aureus, which are common causes of infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 100 grams of fresh phyllanthus niruri leaves and flowers at dawn to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material in a shaded area for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Weigh out 10 grams of dried phyllanthus niruri and combine it with 100 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids, resulting in a 10% tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldthread tinctures helps with infection because it contains berberine, a powerful antimicrobial compound that inhibits the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh coptis chinensis rhizomes and roots for optimal potency and quality.
Wash the harvested material thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces and macerate them in 80% vodka for 6 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding solids.
Store the resulting tincture in an amber-colored glass bottle, protecting it from light, at room temperature for 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod tinctures helps with infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which allow it to target and destroy a wide range of bacteria, viruses, and fungi. Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the goldenrod plant contains bioactive compounds that can be extracted into a tincture, which has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest freshly dried solidago virgaurea leaves and stems from late summer to early fall.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using scissors or a knife.
Combine 250 grams of chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which target and destroy harmful bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause illnesses. The tannins present in fennel help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, while the volatile oils work to break down and eliminate the causative agents of infection. This natural approach can provide effective relief from symptoms such as fever, coughing, and congestion, making it a popular remedy for respiratory and gastrointestinal infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Fennel tincture has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacteria, viruses, and fungi, helping to combat infections and prevent their spread.

Seeds: Fennel seeds contain essential oils that exhibit antimicrobial activity, helping to combat bacterial and fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare flowers and leaves in early summer for optimal potency.
Weigh out 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare aerial parts for tincture making.
Combine dried foeniculum vulgare with 400 milliliters of vodka at a ratio of 1:4.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 14 days, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid extract, discarding solids and storing the tincture in dark glass.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi. The tincture's high concentration of flavonoids and polyphenols works synergistically to disrupt the biofilm formation of pathogenic microorganisms, preventing them from establishing a stronghold within the body. Additionally, nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and edema, promoting healthy tissue repair and minimizing tissue damage caused by infection. Antimicrobial properties: The bioactive compounds in stinging nettle tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, exhibit antimicrobial activity against a wide range of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, making it an effective natural antibacterial agent for wound care and preventing secondary infections.

Antioxidant properties: Stinging nettle tincture's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate infection-related tissue damage and oxidative stress, promoting faster recovery and reducing the risk of chronic inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against bacterial infections.
Seeds: Stinging nettle seeds contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them useful in treating skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves or purchase dried stinging nettle leaf from a reputable source in sufficient quantities for the desired amount of tincture.
Combine one part dried stinging nettle leaf with two parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator at 40°f (4°c) for up to two years, shaking well before each use.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair Tree tincture's antioxidant compounds help combat free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to infection and inflammation in the body.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with infections, while also promoting healing and tissue repair.

Antimicrobial properties: Maidenhair Tree's antimicrobial compounds exhibit broad-spectrum activity against various microorganisms, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi, making it effective in treating and preventing infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating infections.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia, which are often caused by infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in early morning when dew is still present on the leaves for optimal potency and quality.
Chop the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 200g of chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 400ml of 50% ethanol-based vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete coverage of the herb.
Store the jar at room temperature for six weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate maceration and allow the active compounds to dissolve in the solvent.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and preserving the tincture for further use and storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which allow it to target and eliminate bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause infections. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to inhibit the growth of pathogens, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals, preventing further damage and promoting the body's natural defense against infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Turmeric's antimicrobial activity helps combat bacterial, viral, and fungal infections by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms, reducing the risk of infection and promoting recovery.

Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation, which is often a precursor to infection, and also help alleviate symptoms such as pain, swelling, and redness associated with infection.

Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage tissues and cells, potentially reducing the severity of infections by preventing oxidative stress and promoting immune function.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, also known as the underground stem, contains a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat infections.
Roots: Turmeric roots are rich in volatile oils, including turmerone and atlantone, which exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities, making them effective against various types of infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric root and rinse thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the turmeric root in a warm dry place for at least 24 hours or until brittle.
Grind 100 grams of dried turmeric root into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 100 grams of ground turmeric root with 500 milliliters of vodka and store in an amber glass jar for four weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids, transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with infection because of its potent antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as brahmin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of microorganisms, reducing the severity of infections. Brahmi tincture's ability to modulate the immune system also enhances the body's natural defenses against infection, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with infections, further supporting its therapeutic benefits in the treatment of various infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as brahmoside and centaureidin, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, making it an effective remedy for treating infections that cause pain and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in Brahmi, including flavonoids and saponins, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, helping to combat infections and promote healing.

Immune-boosting properties: Brahmi's immune-modulating properties, which include increasing the production of cytokines and activating natural killer cells, help stimulate the body's natural defense against infection, making it an effective adjunctive therapy for treating infectious diseases.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Brahmi are rich in alkaloids and saponins, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties making them effective against various types of infections.
Rhyzomes: Brahmi's rhyzomes contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in infected areas.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried bacopa monnieri leaves and 400ml of high-proof vodka or 95% ethanol.
Combine the bacopa monnieri leaves with 400ml of water in a clean glass jar to create a 25:200 ratio.
Steep the mixture for 4 weeks at room temperature, shaking daily, to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool, dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which allow it to target and eliminate harmful bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause illness. The tannins and flavonoids present in valerian root have been shown to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, making it an effective natural remedy for combating infections such as sinusitis, bronchitis, and urinary tract infections. Additionally, valerian's ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation can also help alleviate symptoms associated with infection, promoting a faster recovery process.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Valerian root has been shown to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi, making it effective against infections caused by these pathogens.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with infections, such as abscesses or wound infections.
Antioxidant activity: Valerian's antioxidant properties can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by infections, which can damage cells and tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots have been traditionally used for centuries to treat various infections, including urinary tract infections and respiratory issues, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizome of the valerian plant contains a high concentration of bioactive compounds like isovaleric acid, which helps to combat infections by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after dew has dried, ensuring 100 grams of fresh material.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces, allowing for efficient infusion and minimizing waste.
Combine chopped roots with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or rum, filling a clean glass jar to the brim.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking occasionally to facilitate extraction and tincture formation.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding solids and reserving the liquid as the finished valeriana officinalis tincture.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to infection that are treated with herbal tinctures are fungal infections, viral infections, and bacterial infections. Herbal tinctures like goldenseal, echinacea, and tea tree oil are effective against candida overgrowth, herpes simplex, and MRSA infections. Other examples include tinctures made from garlic, ginger, and turmeric, which can help combat sinusitis, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues caused by infection or inflammation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with infection is Echinacea and Goldenseal. Echinacea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms. Goldenseal, on the other hand, contains berberine, a compound that has antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacterial and fungal infections. Combining these two tinctures can provide a powerful defense against infection, promoting healing and immune system function. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal tinctures.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Forschende Komplementarmedizin und klassische Naturheilkunde = Research in complementary and natural classical medicine, herbal tinctures have been found to be effective against infections caused by Gram-positive bacteria. The study, which tested the antibacterial properties of 5 herbal drugs and PADMA 28, a preparation of 20 herbs, found that the ethanolic PADMA 28 tinctures exhibited an improved inhibitory effect on Gram-positive bacteria, with minimum inhibitory concentrations (MIC) ranging from 0.38-1.51% tincture or 0.38-1.51 mg PADMA 28/ml. This suggests that herbal tinctures can be a useful treatment option for infections caused by Gram-positive bacteria, although further research is needed to explore their effectiveness against other types of bacteria and fungi.
